plan_metadata.name: Feature Simple Bot Single Progress Bar ts Remote Debug

steps.description: Click on the "Other Teams Capability" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the programming language selection dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Default folder" option (/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects) in the "Workspace Folder" dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Invoke the "Command Palette" in Visual Studio Code by pressing the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace interface.
Type the text 'provision' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field to search for the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command.

Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette, as indicated by the highlighted blue selection and red crosshair in the search bar at the top of the VS Code interface.
Click the "+ New resource group" option in the "Select a resource group" dropdown within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the Enter key in the Visual Studio Code input dialog for "New resource group name" with the value entered.
Click the "Central US" option in the Azure region selection dropdown within the "Location for the new resource group" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Provision" button in the Azure account and subscription selection dialog within Visual Studio Code to initiate resource provisioning for the dev environment.
@assertion It only has one progress bar showing the actions. The content of progress bar is like "Provision: [*/*] Deploying the ARM templates to Azure"
Open the command palette in Visual Studio Code by pressing **Ctrl+Shift+P** to access global commands and features from the main editor interface.
Type "show notifications" into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Click the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette to display notifications.
@assertion "actions in provision stage executed successfully" exists

Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette from the main editor interface, enabling access to available commands and actions within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Type the text 'deploy' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Deploy" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette to initiate the deploy workflow.
Click the "Deploy" button in the Visual Studio Code confirmation dialog asking "Do you want to deploy resources in dev environment?"
@assertion It only has one progress bar showing the actions. The content of progress bar is like "Deploy: [*/*] Deploying"

Use the keyboard shortcut **Ctrl+Shift+P** to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, as shown in the main editor window with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension active and the integrated terminal visible at the bottom.
Type 'show notifications' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top of the interface.
Click the "Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette, highlighted after searching for ">show notifications".
@assertion "actions in deploy stage executed successfully" exists
Use keyboard shortcut `Ctrl+Shift+P` in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette from the main application window, with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension active in the left sidebar.
Type 'microsoft 365 agents:publish' into the VS Code Command Palette to trigger the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Publish" command.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Publish" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette to initiate the publish workflow for Microsoft 365 Agents.
@assertion It only has one progress bar showing the actions. The content of progress bar is like "Publish: [*/*] Publishing app"
